name: abs
doc: Returns the absolute value of x.

name: acos
doc: Returns the arc cosine of x.

name: all_match
doc: Returns whether all elements of an array match the given predicate. Returns true if all the elements match the predicate (a special case is when the array is empty); false if one or more elements don’t match; NULL if the predicate function returns NULL for one or more elements and true for all other

name: any_match
doc: Returns whether any elements of an array match the given predicate. Returns true if one or more elements match the predicate; false if none of the elements matches (a special case is when the array is empty); NULL if the predicate function returns NULL for one or more elements and false for all othe

name: any_value
doc: Returns an arbitrary non-null value x, if one exists. x can be any valid expression. This allows you to return values from columns that are not directly part of the aggregation, including expressions using these columns, in a query.

name: approx_distinct
doc: Returns the approximate number of distinct input values. This function provides an approximation of count(DISTINCT x). Zero is returned if all input values are null.

name: approx_most_frequent
signature: approx_most_frequent(buckets, value, capacity) → map<[same as value], bigint>
doc: Computes the top frequent values up to buckets elements approximately. Approximate estimation of the function enables us to pick up the frequent values with less memory. Larger capacity improves the accuracy of underlying algorithm with sacrificing the memory capacity. The returned value is a map co

name: approx_percentile
doc: Returns the approximate percentile for all input values of x at the given percentage. The value of percentage must be between zero and one and must be constant for all input rows.

name: approx_set
doc: Returns the HyperLogLog sketch of the input data set of x. This data sketch underlies approx_distinct() and can be stored and used later by calling cardinality().

name: arbitrary
doc: Returns an arbitrary non-null value of x, if one exists. Identical to any_value().

name: array_agg
doc: Returns an array created from the input x elements.

name: array_distinct
doc: Remove duplicate values from the array x.

name: array_except
doc: Returns an array of elements in x but not in y, without duplicates.

name: array_first
doc: Returns the first element of an array. If the array is empty, the function returns NULL, whereas the subscript operator would fail in such a case.

name: array_histogram
doc: Returns a map where the keys are the unique elements in the input array x and the values are the number of times that each element appears in x. Null values are ignored.

name: array_intersect
doc: Returns an array of the elements in the intersection of x and y, without duplicates.

name: array_join
doc: Concatenates the elements of the given array using the delimiter. Null elements are omitted in the result.

name: array_last
doc: Returns the last element of an array. If the array is empty, the function returns NULL, whereas the subscript operator would fail in such a case.

name: array_max
doc: Returns the maximum value of input array.

name: array_min
doc: Returns the minimum value of input array.

name: array_position
doc: Returns the position of the first occurrence of the element in array x (or 0 if not found).

name: array_remove
doc: Remove all elements that equal element from array x.

name: array_sort
doc: Sorts and returns the array x. The elements of x must be orderable. Null elements will be placed at the end of the returned array.

name: array_union
doc: Returns an array of the elements in the union of x and y, without duplicates.

name: arrays_overlap
doc: Tests if arrays x and y have any non-null elements in common. Returns null if there are no non-null elements in common but either array contains null.

name: asin
doc: Returns the arc sine of x.

name: at_timezone
signature: at_timezone(x, zone) → timestamp(p) with time zone
doc: Converts x to a time zone specified in zone.

name: atan
doc: Returns the arc tangent of x.

name: atan2
doc: Returns the arc tangent of y / x.

name: avg
doc: Returns the average (arithmetic mean) of all input values.

name: bar
doc: Renders a single bar in an ANSI bar chart using a default low_color of red and a high_color of green. For example, if x of 25% and width of 40 are passed to this function. A 10-character red bar will be drawn followed by 30 spaces to create a bar of 40 characters.

name: beta_cdf
doc: Compute the Beta cdf with given a, b parameters: P(N < v; a, b). The a, b parameters must be positive real numbers and value v must be a real value. The value v must lie on the interval [0, 1].

name: bing_tile
doc: Creates a Bing tile object from XY coordinates and a zoom level. Zoom levels from 1 to 23 are supported.

name: bing_tile_at
doc: Returns a Bing tile at a given zoom level containing a point at a given latitude and longitude. Latitude must be within [-85.05112878, 85.05112878] range. Longitude must be within [-180, 180] range. Zoom levels from 1 to 23 are supported.

name: bing_tile_coordinates
doc: Returns the XY coordinates of a given Bing tile.

name: bing_tile_polygon
doc: Returns the polygon representation of a given Bing tile.

name: bing_tile_quadkey
doc: Returns the quadkey of a given Bing tile.

name: bing_tile_zoom_level
doc: Returns the zoom level of a given Bing tile.

name: bing_tiles_around
doc: Returns a collection of Bing tiles that surround the point specified by the latitude and longitude arguments at a given zoom level.

name: bit_count
doc: Count the number of bits set in x (treated as bits-bit signed integer) in 2’s complement representation:

name: bitwise_and
doc: Returns the bitwise AND of x and y in 2’s complement representation.

name: bitwise_and_agg
doc: Returns the bitwise AND of all input non-NULL values in 2’s complement representation. If all records inside the group are NULL, or if the group is empty, the function returns NULL.

name: bitwise_left_shift
doc: Returns the left shifted value of value.

name: bitwise_not
doc: Returns the bitwise NOT of x in 2’s complement representation (NOT x = -x - 1):

name: bitwise_or
doc: Returns the bitwise OR of x and y in 2’s complement representation.

name: bitwise_or_agg
doc: Returns the bitwise OR of all input non-NULL values in 2’s complement representation. If all records inside the group are NULL, or if the group is empty, the function returns NULL.

name: bitwise_right_shift
doc: Returns the logical right shifted value of value.

name: bitwise_right_shift_arithmetic
doc: Returns the arithmetic right shifted value of value.

name: bitwise_xor
doc: Returns the bitwise XOR of x and y in 2’s complement representation.

name: bitwise_xor_agg
doc: Returns the bitwise XOR of all input non-NULL values in 2’s complement representation. If all records inside the group are NULL, or if the group is empty, the function returns NULL.

name: bool_and
doc: Returns TRUE if every input value is TRUE, otherwise FALSE.

name: bool_or
doc: Returns TRUE if any input value is TRUE, otherwise FALSE.

name: cardinality
doc: Returns the cardinality (size) of the array x.

name: cast
doc: Explicitly cast a value as a type. This can be used to cast a varchar to a numeric value type and vice versa.

name: cbrt
doc: Returns the cube root of x.

name: ceil
doc: This is an alias for ceiling().

name: ceiling
doc: Returns x rounded up to the nearest integer.

name: char2hexint
doc: Returns the hexadecimal representation of the UTF-16BE encoding of the string.

name: checksum
doc: Returns an order-insensitive checksum of the given values.

name: chr
doc: Returns the Unicode code point n as a single character string.

name: codepoint
doc: Returns the Unicode code point of the only character of string.

name: color
doc: Returns a color capturing a decoded RGB value from a 4-character string of the format “#000”. The input string should be varchar containing a CSS-style short rgb string or one of black, red, green, yellow, blue, magenta, cyan, white.

name: combinations
doc: Returns n-element sub-groups of input array. If the input array has no duplicates, combinations returns n-element subsets.

name: concat
doc: Returns the concatenation of string1, string2, ..., stringN. This function provides the same functionality as the SQL-standard concatenation operator (||).

name: concat_ws
doc: Returns the concatenation of string1, string2, ..., stringN using separator to join the values. If separator is null, then the return value is null. Any null values provided in the arguments after the separator are skipped.

name: contains
doc: Returns true if the array x contains the element.

name: contains_sequence
doc: Return true if array x contains all of array seq as a subsequence (all values in the same consecutive order).

name: convex_hull_agg
doc: Returns the minimum convex geometry that encloses all input geometries.

name: corr
doc: Returns correlation coefficient of input values.

name: cos
doc: Returns the cosine of x.

name: cosh
doc: Returns the hyperbolic cosine of x.

name: cosine_distance
doc: Calculates the cosine distance between two dense vectors:

name: cosine_similarity
doc: Calculates the cosine similarity of two dense vectors:

name: count
doc: Returns the number of input rows.

name: count_if
doc: Returns the number of TRUE input values. This function is equivalent to count(CASE WHEN x THEN 1 END).

name: covar_pop
doc: Returns the population covariance of input values.

name: covar_samp
doc: Returns the sample covariance of input values.

name: crc32
doc: Computes the CRC-32 of binary. For general purpose hashing, use xxhash64(), as it is much faster and produces a better quality hash.

name: cume_dist
doc: Returns the cumulative distribution of a value in a group of values. The result is the number of rows preceding or peer with the row in the window ordering of the window partition divided by the total number of rows in the window partition. Thus, any tie values in the ordering will evaluate to the s

name: current_groups
doc: Returns the list of groups for the current user running the query.

name: current_timezone
doc: Returns the current time zone in the format defined by IANA (e.g., America/Los_Angeles) or as fixed offset from UTC (e.g., +08:35)

name: date
doc: This is an alias for CAST(x AS date).

name: date_add
doc: Adds an interval value of type unit to timestamp. Subtraction can be performed by using a negative value:

name: date_diff
doc: Returns timestamp2 - timestamp1 expressed in terms of unit:

name: date_format
doc: Formats timestamp as a string using format:

name: date_trunc
doc: Returns x truncated to unit:

name: day
doc: Returns the day of the month from x.

name: day_of_month
doc: This is an alias for day().

name: day_of_week
doc: Returns the ISO day of the week from x. The value ranges from 1 (Monday) to 7 (Sunday).

name: day_of_year
doc: Returns the day of the year from x. The value ranges from 1 to 366.

name: degrees
doc: Converts angle x in radians to degrees.

name: dense_rank
doc: Returns the rank of a value in a group of values. This is similar to rank(), except that tie values do not produce gaps in the sequence. The window frame must not be specified.

name: dot_product
doc: Calculates the dot product:

name: dow
doc: This is an alias for day_of_week().

name: doy
doc: This is an alias for day_of_year().

name: e
doc: Returns the constant Euler’s number.

name: element_at
doc: Returns element of array at given index. If index > 0, this function provides the same functionality as the SQL-standard subscript operator ([]), except that the function returns NULL when accessing an index larger than array length, whereas the subscript operator would fail in such a case. If index

name: empty_approx_set
doc: Returns an empty HyperLogLog.

name: ends_with
doc: Tests whether substring is a suffix of string.

name: euclidean_distance
doc: Calculates the euclidean distance:

name: every
doc: This is an alias for bool_and().

name: exp
doc: Returns Euler’s number raised to the power of x.

name: extract
doc: Returns field from x:

name: filter
doc: Constructs an array from those elements of array for which function returns true:

name: first_value
doc: Returns the first value of the window.

name: flatten
doc: Flattens an array(array(T)) to an array(T) by concatenating the contained arrays.

name: floor
doc: Returns x rounded down to the nearest integer.

name: format
doc: Returns a formatted string using the specified format string and arguments:

name: format_datetime
doc: Formats timestamp as a string using format.

name: format_number
doc: Returns a formatted string using a unit symbol:

name: from_base
doc: Returns the value of string interpreted as a base-radix number.

name: from_base32
doc: Decodes binary data from the base32 encoded string.

name: from_base64
doc: Decodes binary data from the base64 encoded string.

name: from_base64url
doc: Decodes binary data from the base64 encoded string using the URL safe alphabet.

name: from_big_endian_32
doc: Decodes the 32-bit two’s complement big-endian binary. The input must be exactly 4 bytes.

name: from_big_endian_64
doc: Decodes the 64-bit two’s complement big-endian binary. The input must be exactly 8 bytes.

name: from_encoded_polyline
doc: Decodes a polyline to a linestring.

name: from_geojson_geometry
doc: Returns the spherical geography type object from the GeoJSON representation stripping non geometry key/values. Feature and FeatureCollection are not supported.

name: from_hex
doc: Decodes binary data from the hex encoded string.

name: from_ieee754_32
doc: Decodes the 32-bit big-endian binary in IEEE 754 single-precision floating-point format. The input must be exactly 4 bytes.

name: from_ieee754_64
doc: Decodes the 64-bit big-endian binary in IEEE 754 double-precision floating-point format. The input must be exactly 8 bytes.

name: from_iso8601_date
doc: Parses the ISO 8601 formatted date string into a date. The date can be a calendar date, a week date using ISO week numbering, or year and day of year combined:

name: from_iso8601_timestamp
doc: Parses the ISO 8601 formatted date string, optionally with time and time zone, into a timestamp(3) with time zone. The time defaults to 00:00:00.000, and the time zone defaults to the session time zone:

name: from_iso8601_timestamp_nanos
doc: Parses the ISO 8601 formatted date and time string. The time zone defaults to the session time zone:

name: from_unixtime
doc: Returns the UNIX timestamp unixtime as a timestamp with time zone. unixtime is the number of seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC.

name: from_unixtime_nanos
doc: Returns the UNIX timestamp unixtime as a timestamp with time zone. unixtime is the number of nanoseconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00.000000000 UTC:

name: from_utf8
doc: Decodes a UTF-8 encoded string from binary. Invalid UTF-8 sequences are replaced with the Unicode replacement character U+FFFD.

name: geometric_mean
doc: Returns the geometric mean of all input values.

name: geometry_from_hadoop_shape
doc: Returns a geometry type object from Spatial Framework for Hadoop representation.

name: geometry_invalid_reason
doc: Returns the reason for why the input geometry is not valid. Returns NULL if the input is valid.

name: geometry_nearest_points
doc: Returns the points on each geometry nearest the other. If either geometry is empty, return NULL. Otherwise, return a row of two Points that have the minimum distance of any two points on the geometries. The first Point will be from the first Geometry argument, the second from the second Geometry arg

name: geometry_to_bing_tiles
doc: Returns the minimum set of Bing tiles that fully covers a given geometry at a given zoom level. Zoom levels from 1 to 23 are supported.

name: geometry_union
doc: Returns a geometry that represents the point set union of the input geometries. Performance of this function, in conjunction with array_agg() to first aggregate the input geometries, may be better than geometry_union_agg(), at the expense of higher memory utilization.

name: geometry_union_agg
doc: Returns a geometry that represents the point set union of all input geometries.

name: great_circle_distance
doc: Returns the great-circle distance between two points on Earth’s surface in kilometers.

name: greatest
doc: Returns the largest of the provided values.

name: hamming_distance
doc: Returns the Hamming distance of string1 and string2, i.e. the number of positions at which the corresponding characters are different. Note that the two strings must have the same length.

name: hash_counts
doc: Returns a map containing the Murmur3Hash128 hashed values and the count of their occurences within the internal MinHash structure belonging to x.

name: histogram
doc: Returns a map containing the count of the number of times each input value occurs.

name: hmac_md5
doc: Computes HMAC with MD5 of binary with the given key.

name: hmac_sha1
doc: Computes HMAC with SHA1 of binary with the given key.

name: hmac_sha256
doc: Computes HMAC with SHA256 of binary with the given key.

name: hmac_sha512
doc: Computes HMAC with SHA512 of binary with the given key.

name: hour
doc: Returns the hour of the day from x. The value ranges from 0 to 23.

name: human_readable_seconds
doc: Formats the double value of seconds into a human-readable string containing weeks, days, hours, minutes, and seconds:

name: id0
doc: Evaluates and returns true_value if condition is true, otherwise null is returned and true_value is not evaluated.

name: id1
doc: Returns the first non-null value in the argument list. Like a CASE expression, arguments are only evaluated if necessary.

name: id2
doc: Returns null if value1 equals value2, otherwise returns value1.

name: id3
doc: Evaluate an expression and handle certain types of errors by returning NULL.

name: index
doc: Alias for strpos() function.

name: infinity
doc: Returns the constant representing positive infinity.

name: intersection_cardinality
doc: Returns the estimation for the cardinality of the intersection of the two set digests.

name: inverse_beta_cdf
doc: Compute the inverse of the Beta cdf with given a, b parameters for the cumulative probability (p): P(N < n). The a, b parameters must be positive real values. The probability p must lie on the interval [0, 1].

name: inverse_normal_cdf
doc: Compute the inverse of the Normal cdf with given mean and standard deviation (sd) for the cumulative probability (p): P(N < n). The mean must be a real value and the standard deviation must be a real and positive value. The probability p must lie on the interval (0, 1).

name: is_finite
doc: Determine if x is finite.

name: is_infinite
doc: Determine if x is infinite.

name: is_json_scalar
doc: Determine if json is a scalar (i.e. a JSON number, a JSON string, true, false or null):

name: is_nan
doc: Determine if x is not-a-number.

name: jaccard_index
doc: Returns the estimation of Jaccard index for the two set digests.

name: json_array_contains
doc: Determine if value exists in json (a string containing a JSON array):

name: json_array_get
doc: Warning

name: json_array_length
doc: Returns the array length of json (a string containing a JSON array):

name: json_extract
doc: Evaluates the JSONPath-like expression json_path on json (a string containing JSON) and returns the result as a JSON string:

name: json_extract_scalar
doc: Like json_extract(), but returns the result value as a string (as opposed to being encoded as JSON). The value referenced by json_path must be a scalar (boolean, number or string).

name: json_format
doc: Returns the JSON text serialized from the input JSON value. This is inverse function to json_parse().

name: json_parse
doc: Returns the JSON value deserialized from the input JSON text. This is inverse function to json_format():

name: json_size
doc: Like json_extract(), but returns the size of the value. For objects or arrays, the size is the number of members, and the size of a scalar value is zero.

name: kurtosis
doc: Returns the excess kurtosis of all input values. Unbiased estimate using the following expression:

name: lag
doc: Returns the value at offset rows before the current row in the window partition. Offsets start at 0, which is the current row. The offset can be any scalar expression. The default offset is 1. If the offset is null, an error is raised. If the offset refers to a row that is not within the partition,

name: last_day_of_month
doc: Returns the last day of the month.

name: last_value
doc: Returns the last value of the window.

name: lead
doc: Returns the value at offset rows after the current row in the window partition. Offsets start at 0, which is the current row. The offset can be any scalar expression. The default offset is 1. If the offset is null, an error is raised. If the offset refers to a row that is not within the partition, t

name: least
doc: Returns the smallest of the provided values.

name: length
doc: Returns the length of string in characters.

name: levenshtein_distance
doc: Returns the Levenshtein edit distance of string1 and string2, i.e. the minimum number of single-character edits (insertions, deletions or substitutions) needed to change string1 into string2.

name: line_interpolate_point
doc: Returns a Point interpolated along a LineString at the fraction given. The fraction must be between 0 and 1, inclusive.

name: line_interpolate_points
doc: Returns an array of Points interpolated along a LineString. The fraction must be between 0 and 1, inclusive.

name: line_locate_point
doc: Returns a float between 0 and 1 representing the location of the closest point on the LineString to the given Point, as a fraction of total 2d line length.

name: listagg
doc: Returns the concatenated input values, separated by the separator string.

name: ln
doc: Returns the natural logarithm of x.

name: log
doc: Returns the base b logarithm of x.

name: log10
doc: Returns the base 10 logarithm of x.

name: log2
doc: Returns the base 2 logarithm of x.

name: lower
doc: Converts string to lowercase.

name: lpad
doc: Left pads string to size characters with padstring. If size is less than the length of string, the result is truncated to size characters. size must not be negative and padstring must be non-empty.

name: ltrim
doc: Removes leading whitespace from string.

name: luhn_check
doc: Tests whether a string of digits is valid according to the Luhn algorithm.

name: make_set_digest
doc: Composes all input values of x into a setdigest.

name: map
doc: Returns an empty map.

name: map_agg
doc: Returns a map created from the input key / value pairs.

name: map_concat
signature: map_concat(map1(K, V), map2(K, V), ..., mapN(K, V)) → map(K, V)
doc: Returns the union of all the given maps. If a key is found in multiple given maps, that key’s value in the resulting map comes from the last one of those maps.

name: map_entries
doc: Returns an array of all entries in the given map.

name: map_filter
signature: map_filter(map(K, V), function(K, V, boolean)) → map(K, V)
doc: Constructs a map from those entries of map for which function returns true:

name: map_from_entries
doc: Returns a map created from the given array of entries.

name: map_keys
doc: Returns all the keys in the map x.

name: map_union
doc: Returns the union of all the input maps. If a key is found in multiple input maps, that key’s value in the resulting map comes from an arbitrary input map.

name: map_values
doc: Returns all the values in the map x.

name: map_zip_with
signature: map_zip_with(map(K, V1), map(K, V2), function(K, V1, V2, V3)) → map(K, V3)
doc: Merges the two given maps into a single map by applying function to the pair of values with the same key. For keys only presented in one map, NULL will be passed as the value for the missing key.

name: max
doc: Returns the maximum value of all input values.

name: max_by
doc: Returns the value of x associated with the maximum value of y over all input values.

name: md5
doc: Computes the MD5 hash of binary.

name: merge
doc: Returns the HyperLogLog of the aggregate union of the individual hll HyperLogLog structures.

name: merge_set_digest
doc: Returns the setdigest of the aggregate union of the individual setdigest Set Digest structures.

name: millisecond
doc: Returns the millisecond of the second from x.

name: min
doc: Returns the minimum value of all input values.

name: min_by
doc: Returns the value of x associated with the minimum value of y over all input values.

name: minute
doc: Returns the minute of the hour from x.

name: mod
doc: Returns the modulo (remainder) of n divided by m.

name: month
doc: Returns the month of the year from x.

name: multimap_agg
doc: Returns a multimap created from the input key / value pairs. Each key can be associated with multiple values.

name: multimap_from_entries
doc: Returns a multimap created from the given array of entries. Each key can be associated with multiple values.

name: murmur3
doc: Computes the 128-bit MurmurHash3 hash of binary.

name: nan
doc: Returns the constant representing not-a-number.

name: ngrams
doc: Returns n-grams (sub-sequences of adjacent n elements) for the array. The order of the n-grams in the result is unspecified.

name: none_match
doc: Returns whether no elements of an array match the given predicate. Returns true if none of the elements matches the predicate (a special case is when the array is empty); false if one or more elements match; NULL if the predicate function returns NULL for one or more elements and false for all other

name: normal_cdf
doc: Compute the Normal cdf with given mean and standard deviation (sd): P(N < v; mean, sd). The mean and value v must be real values and the standard deviation must be a real and positive value.

name: normalize
doc: Transforms string with NFC normalization form.

name: now
doc: This is an alias for current_timestamp.

name: nth_value
doc: Returns the value at the specified offset from the beginning of the window. Offsets start at 1. The offset can be any scalar expression. If the offset is null or greater than the number of values in the window, null is returned. It is an error for the offset to be zero or negative.

name: ntile
doc: Divides the rows for each window partition into n buckets ranging from 1 to at most n. Bucket values will differ by at most 1. If the number of rows in the partition does not divide evenly into the number of buckets, then the remainder values are distributed one per bucket, starting with the first b

name: numeric_histogram
doc: Computes an approximate histogram with up to buckets number of buckets for all values with a per-item weight of weight. The algorithm is based loosely on:

name: overlay
signature: overlay(string PLACING replacement FROM start) → varchar
doc: Returns string with the substring beginning at position start (1-based, must be greater than 0) replaced by replacement. The number of characters removed defaults to the length of replacement. The result keeps the same length as string only when the replaced range lies entirely within string; when t

name: parse_data_size
doc: Parses string of format value unit into a number, where value is the fractional number of unit values:

name: parse_datetime
signature: parse_datetime(string, format) → timestamp with time zone
doc: Parses string into a timestamp with time zone using format.

name: parse_duration
doc: Parses string of format value unit into an interval, where value is fractional number of unit values:

name: percent_rank
doc: Returns the percentage ranking of a value in group of values. The result is (r - 1) / (n - 1) where r is the rank() of the row and n is the total number of rows in the window partition. The window frame must not be specified.

name: pi
doc: Returns the constant Pi.

name: position
doc: Returns the starting position of the first instance of substring in string. Positions start with 1. If not found, 0 is returned.

name: pow
doc: This is an alias for power().

name: power
doc: Returns x raised to the power of p.

name: quarter
doc: Returns the quarter of the year from x. The value ranges from 1 to 4.

name: radians
doc: Converts angle x in degrees to radians.

name: rand
doc: This is an alias for random().

name: random
doc: Returns a pseudo-random value in the range 0.0 <= x < 1.0.

name: rank
doc: Returns the rank of a value in a group of values. The rank is one plus the number of rows preceding the row that are not peer with the row. Thus, tie values in the ordering will produce gaps in the sequence. The ranking is performed for each window partition. The window frame must not be specified.

name: reduce
signature: reduce(array(T), initialState S, inputFunction(S, T, S), outputFunction(S, R)) → R
doc: Returns a single value reduced from array. inputFunction will be invoked for each element in array in order. In addition to taking the element, inputFunction takes the current state, initially initialState, and returns the new state. outputFunction will be invoked to turn the final state into the re

name: reduce_agg
signature: reduce_agg(inputValue T, initialState S, inputFunction(S, T, S), combineFunction(S, S, S)) → S
doc: Reduces all input values into a single value. inputFunction will be invoked for each non-null input value. In addition to taking the input value, inputFunction takes the current state, initially initialState, and returns the new state. combineFunction will be invoked to combine two states into a new

name: regexp_count
doc: Returns the number of occurrence of pattern in string:

name: regexp_extract
doc: Returns the first substring matched by the regular expression pattern in string:

name: regexp_extract_all
doc: Returns the substring(s) matched by the regular expression pattern in string:

name: regexp_like
doc: Evaluates the regular expression pattern and determines if it is contained within string.

name: regexp_position
doc: Returns the index of the first occurrence (counting from 1) of pattern in string. Returns -1 if not found:

name: regexp_replace
doc: Removes every instance of the substring matched by the regular expression pattern from string:

name: regexp_split
doc: Splits string using the regular expression pattern and returns an array. Trailing empty strings are preserved:

name: regr_intercept
doc: Returns linear regression intercept of input values. y is the dependent value. x is the independent value.

name: regr_slope
doc: Returns linear regression slope of input values. y is the dependent value. x is the independent value.

name: render
doc: Renders value x using the specific color using ANSI color codes. x can be either a double, bigint, or varchar.

name: repeat
doc: Repeat element for count times.

name: replace
doc: Removes all instances of search from string.

name: reverse
doc: Returns string with the characters in reverse order.

name: rgb
doc: Returns a color value capturing the RGB value of three component color values supplied as int parameters ranging from 0 to 255: red, green, blue.

name: round
doc: Returns x rounded to the nearest integer.

name: row_number
doc: Returns a unique, sequential number for each row, starting with one, according to the ordering of rows within the window partition. The window frame must not be specified.

name: rpad
doc: Right pads string to size characters with padstring. If size is less than the length of string, the result is truncated to size characters. size must not be negative and padstring must be non-empty.

name: rtrim
doc: Removes trailing whitespace from string.

name: second
doc: Returns the second of the minute from x.

name: sequence
doc: Generate a sequence of integers from start to stop, incrementing by 1 if start is less than or equal to stop, otherwise -1.

name: sha1
doc: Computes the SHA1 hash of binary.

name: sha256
doc: Computes the SHA256 hash of binary.

name: sha512
doc: Computes the SHA512 hash of binary.

name: shuffle
doc: Generate a random permutation of the given array x.

name: sign
doc: Returns the signum function of x, that is:

name: simplify_geometry
doc: Returns a “simplified” version of the input geometry using the Douglas-Peucker algorithm. Will avoid creating derived geometries (polygons in particular) that are invalid.

name: sin
doc: Returns the sine of x.

name: sinh
doc: Returns the hyperbolic sine of x.

name: skewness
doc: Returns the Fisher’s moment coefficient of skewness of all input values.

name: slice
doc: Subsets array x starting from index start (or starting from the end if start is negative) with a length of length.

name: soundex
doc: It is typically used to evaluate the similarity of two expressions phonetically, that is how the string sounds when spoken:

name: split
doc: Splits string on delimiter and returns an array.

name: split_part
doc: Splits string on delimiter and returns the field index. Field indexes start with 1. If the index is larger than the number of fields, then null is returned.

name: split_to_map
doc: Splits string by entryDelimiter and keyValueDelimiter and returns a map. entryDelimiter splits string into key-value pairs. keyValueDelimiter splits each pair into key and value.

name: split_to_multimap
doc: Splits string by entryDelimiter and keyValueDelimiter and returns a map containing an array of values for each unique key. entryDelimiter splits string into key-value pairs. keyValueDelimiter splits each pair into key and value. The values for each key will be in the same order as they appeared in s

name: spooky_hash_v2_32
doc: Computes the 32-bit SpookyHashV2 hash of binary.

name: spooky_hash_v2_64
doc: Computes the 64-bit SpookyHashV2 hash of binary.

name: sqrt
doc: Returns the square root of x.

name: st_area
doc: Returns the 2D Euclidean area of a geometry.

name: st_asbinary
doc: Returns the WKB representation of the geometry.

name: st_astext
doc: Returns the WKT representation of the geometry. For empty geometries, ST_AsText(ST_LineFromText('LINESTRING EMPTY')) will produce 'MULTILINESTRING EMPTY' and ST_AsText(ST_Polygon('POLYGON EMPTY')) will produce 'MULTIPOLYGON EMPTY'.

name: st_boundary
doc: Returns the closure of the combinatorial boundary of this geometry.

name: st_buffer
doc: Returns the geometry that represents all points whose distance from the specified geometry is less than or equal to the specified distance. If the points of the geometry are extremely close together (delta < 1e-8), this might return an empty geometry.

name: st_centroid
doc: Returns the point value that is the mathematical centroid of a geometry.

name: st_contains
doc: Returns true if and only if no points of the second geometry lie in the exterior of the first geometry, and at least one point of the interior of the first geometry lies in the interior of the second geometry.

name: st_convexhull
doc: Returns the minimum convex geometry that encloses all input geometries.

name: st_coorddim
doc: Returns the coordinate dimension of the geometry.

name: st_crosses
doc: Returns true if the supplied geometries have some, but not all, interior points in common.

name: st_difference
doc: Returns the geometry value that represents the point set difference of the given geometries.

name: st_dimension
doc: Returns the inherent dimension of this geometry object, which must be less than or equal to the coordinate dimension.

name: st_disjoint
doc: Returns true if the give geometries do not spatially intersect – if they do not share any space together.

name: st_distance
doc: Returns the great-circle distance in meters between two SphericalGeography points.

name: st_endpoint
doc: Returns the last point of a LineString geometry as a Point. This is a shortcut for ST_PointN(geometry, ST_NumPoints(geometry)).

name: st_envelope
doc: Returns the bounding rectangular polygon of a geometry.

name: st_envelopeaspts
doc: Returns an array of two points: the lower left and upper right corners of the bounding rectangular polygon of a geometry. Returns NULL if input geometry is empty.

name: st_equals
doc: Returns true if the given geometries represent the same geometry.

name: st_exteriorring
doc: Returns a line string representing the exterior ring of the input polygon.

name: st_geometries
doc: Returns an array of geometries in the specified collection. Returns a one-element array if the input geometry is not a multi-geometry. Returns NULL if input geometry is empty.

name: st_geometryfromtext
doc: Returns a geometry type object from WKT representation.

name: st_geometryn
doc: Returns the geometry element at a given index (indices start at 1). If the geometry is a collection of geometries (e.g., GEOMETRYCOLLECTION or MULTI*), returns the geometry at a given index. If the given index is less than 1 or greater than the total number of elements in the collection, returns NUL

name: st_geometrytype
doc: Returns the type of the geometry.

name: st_geomfrombinary
doc: Returns a geometry type object from WKB or EWKB representation.

name: st_geomfromkml
doc: Returns a geometry type object from KML representation.

name: st_interiorringn
doc: Returns the interior ring element at the specified index (indices start at 1). If the given index is less than 1 or greater than the total number of interior rings in the input geometry, returns NULL. The input geometry must be a polygon. Use ST_NumInteriorRing() to find out the total number of elem

name: st_interiorrings
doc: Returns an array of all interior rings found in the input geometry, or an empty array if the polygon has no interior rings. Returns NULL if the input geometry is empty. The input geometry must be a polygon.

name: st_intersection
doc: Returns the geometry value that represents the point set intersection of two geometries.

name: st_intersects
doc: Returns true if the given geometries spatially intersect in two dimensions (share any portion of space) and false if they do not (they are disjoint).

name: st_isclosed
doc: Returns true if the linestring’s start and end points are coincident.

name: st_isempty
doc: Returns true if this Geometry is an empty geometrycollection, polygon, point etc.

name: st_isring
doc: Returns true if and only if the line is closed and simple.

name: st_issimple
doc: Returns true if this Geometry has no anomalous geometric points, such as self intersection or self tangency.

name: st_isvalid
doc: Returns true if and only if the input geometry is well-formed. Use geometry_invalid_reason() to determine why the geometry is not well-formed.

name: st_length
doc: Returns the length of a linestring or multi-linestring using Euclidean measurement on a two-dimensional plane (based on spatial ref) in projected units.

name: st_linefromtext
doc: Returns a geometry type linestring object from WKT representation.

name: st_linestring
doc: Returns a LineString formed from an array of points. If there are fewer than two non-empty points in the input array, an empty LineString will be returned. Array elements must not be NULL or the same as the previous element. The returned geometry may not be simple, e.g. may self-intersect or may con

name: st_multipoint
doc: Returns a MultiPoint geometry object formed from the specified points. Returns NULL if input array is empty. Array elements must not be NULL or empty. The returned geometry may not be simple and may contain duplicate points if input array has duplicates.

name: st_numgeometries
doc: Returns the number of geometries in the collection. If the geometry is a collection of geometries (e.g., GEOMETRYCOLLECTION or MULTI*), returns the number of geometries, for single geometries returns 1, for empty geometries returns 0.

name: st_numinteriorring
doc: Returns the cardinality of the collection of interior rings of a polygon.

name: st_numpoints
doc: Returns the number of points in a geometry. This is an extension to the SQL/MM ST_NumPoints function which only applies to point and linestring.

name: st_overlaps
doc: Returns true if the given geometries share space, are of the same dimension, but are not completely contained by each other.

name: st_point
doc: Returns a geometry type point object with the given coordinate values.

name: st_pointn
doc: Returns the vertex of a linestring at a given index (indices start at 1). If the given index is less than 1 or greater than the total number of elements in the collection, returns NULL. Use ST_NumPoints() to find out the total number of elements.

name: st_points
doc: Returns an array of points in a linestring.

name: st_polygon
doc: Returns a geometry type polygon object from WKT representation.

name: st_relate
doc: Returns true if first geometry is spatially related to second geometry.

name: st_startpoint
doc: Returns the first point of a LineString geometry as a Point. This is a shortcut for ST_PointN(geometry, 1).

name: st_symdifference
doc: Returns the geometry value that represents the point set symmetric difference of two geometries.

name: st_touches
doc: Returns true if the given geometries have at least one point in common, but their interiors do not intersect.

name: st_union
doc: Returns a geometry that represents the point set union of the input geometries.

name: st_within
doc: Returns true if first geometry is completely inside second geometry.

name: st_x
doc: Returns the X coordinate of the point.

name: st_xmax
doc: Returns X maxima of a bounding box of a geometry.

name: st_xmin
doc: Returns X minima of a bounding box of a geometry.

name: st_y
doc: Returns the Y coordinate of the point.

name: st_ymax
doc: Returns Y maxima of a bounding box of a geometry.

name: st_ymin
doc: Returns Y minima of a bounding box of a geometry.

name: starts_with
doc: Tests whether substring is a prefix of string.

name: stddev
doc: This is an alias for stddev_samp().

name: stddev_pop
doc: Returns the population standard deviation of all input values.

name: stddev_samp
doc: Returns the sample standard deviation of all input values.

name: strpos
doc: Returns the starting position of the first instance of substring in string. Positions start with 1. If not found, 0 is returned.

name: substr
doc: This is an alias for substring().

name: substring
doc: Returns the rest of string from the starting position start. Positions start with 1. A negative starting position is interpreted as being relative to the end of the string.

name: sum
doc: Returns the sum of all input values.

name: t_cdf
doc: Compute the Student’s t-distribution cumulative density function for given x and degrees of freedom (df). The x must be a real value and degrees of freedom must be an integer and positive value.

name: t_pdf
doc: Computes the Student’s t-distribution probability density function for given x and degrees of freedom (df). The x must be a real value and degrees of freedom must be an integer and positive value.

name: tan
doc: Returns the tangent of x.

name: tanh
doc: Returns the hyperbolic tangent of x.

name: tdigest_agg
doc: Composes all input values of x into a tdigest. x can be of any numeric type.

name: theta_sketch_cardinality
doc: Returns the estimated value of the sketch.

name: theta_sketch_union
doc: Returns a serialized sketch as varbinary, which is a merged collection of sketches. The optional nominal_entries and seed parameters let you specify non-default sketch size and seed when merging sketches created with custom settings.

name: timezone
doc: Returns the timezone identifier from timestamp(p) with time zone. The format of the returned identifier is identical to the format used in the input timestamp:

name: timezone_hour
doc: Returns the hour of the time zone offset from timestamp.

name: timezone_minute
doc: Returns the minute of the time zone offset from timestamp.

name: to_base
doc: Returns the base-radix representation of x.

name: to_base32
doc: Encodes binary into a base32 string representation.

name: to_base64
doc: Encodes binary into a base64 string representation.

name: to_base64url
doc: Encodes binary into a base64 string representation using the URL safe alphabet.

name: to_big_endian_32
doc: Encodes integer into a 32-bit two’s complement big-endian format.

name: to_big_endian_64
doc: Encodes bigint into a 64-bit two’s complement big-endian format.

name: to_char
doc: Formats timestamp as a string using format.

name: to_date
doc: Parses string into a DATE using format.

name: to_encoded_polyline
doc: Encodes a linestring or multipoint to a polyline.

name: to_geojson_geometry
doc: Returns the GeoJSON encoded defined by the input spherical geography.

name: to_geometry
doc: Converts a SphericalGeography object to a Geometry object.

name: to_hex
doc: Encodes binary into a hex string representation.

name: to_ieee754_32
doc: Encodes real into a 32-bit big-endian binary according to IEEE 754 single-precision floating-point format.

name: to_ieee754_64
doc: Encodes double into a 64-bit big-endian binary according to IEEE 754 double-precision floating-point format.

name: to_iso8601
doc: Formats x as an ISO 8601 string.

name: to_milliseconds
doc: Returns the day-to-second interval as milliseconds.

name: to_spherical_geography
doc: Converts a Geometry object to a SphericalGeography object on the sphere of the Earth’s radius. This function is only applicable to POINT, MULTIPOINT, LINESTRING, MULTILINESTRING, POLYGON, MULTIPOLYGON geometries defined in 2D space, or GEOMETRYCOLLECTION of such geometries. For each point of the inp

name: to_timestamp
doc: Parses string into a TIMESTAMP using format.

name: to_unixtime
doc: Returns timestamp as a UNIX timestamp.

name: to_utf8
doc: Encodes string into a UTF-8 varbinary representation.

name: transform
doc: Returns an array that is the result of applying function to each element of array:

name: transform_keys
signature: transform_keys(map(K1, V), function(K1, V, K2)) → map(K2, V)
doc: Returns a map that applies function to each entry of map and transforms the keys:

name: transform_values
signature: transform_values(map(K, V1), function(K, V1, V2)) → map(K, V2)
doc: Returns a map that applies function to each entry of map and transforms the values:

name: translate
doc: Returns the source string translated by replacing characters found in the from string with the corresponding characters in the to string. If the from string contains duplicates, only the first is used. If the source character does not exist in the from string, the source character will be copied wit

name: trim
signature: trim([ [ specification ] [ string ] FROM ] source ) → varchar
doc: Removes any leading and/or trailing characters as specified up to and including string from source:

name: trim_array
doc: Remove n elements from the end of array:

name: truncate
doc: Returns x rounded to integer by dropping digits after decimal point.

name: try_cast
doc: Like cast(), but returns null if the cast fails.

name: typeof
doc: Returns the name of the type of the provided expression:

name: upper
doc: Converts string to uppercase.

name: url_decode
doc: Unescapes the URL encoded value. This function is the inverse of url_encode().

name: url_encode
doc: Escapes value by encoding it so that it can be safely included in URL query parameter names and values:

name: url_extract_fragment
doc: Returns the fragment identifier from url.

name: url_extract_host
doc: Returns the host from url.

name: url_extract_parameter
doc: Returns the value of the first query string parameter named name from url. Parameter extraction is handled in the typical manner as specified by RFC 1866#section-8.2.1.

name: url_extract_path
doc: Returns the path from url.

name: url_extract_port
doc: Returns the port number from url.

name: url_extract_protocol
doc: Returns the protocol from url:

name: url_extract_query
doc: Returns the query string from url.

name: uuid
doc: Returns a pseudo randomly generated UUID (type 4).

name: var_pop
doc: Returns the population variance of all input values.

name: var_samp
doc: Returns the sample variance of all input values.

name: variance
doc: This is an alias for var_samp().

name: version
doc: Returns the Trino version used on the cluster. Equivalent to the value of the node_version column in the system.runtime.nodes table.

name: week
doc: Returns the ISO week of the year from x. The value ranges from 1 to 53.

name: week_of_year
doc: This is an alias for week().

name: width_bucket
doc: Returns the bin number of x in an equi-width histogram with the specified bound1 and bound2 bounds and n number of buckets.

name: wilson_interval_lower
doc: Returns the lower bound of the Wilson score interval of a Bernoulli trial process at a confidence specified by the z-score z.

name: wilson_interval_upper
doc: Returns the upper bound of the Wilson score interval of a Bernoulli trial process at a confidence specified by the z-score z.

name: with_timezone
signature: with_timezone(timestamp(p), zone) → timestamp(p) with time zone
doc: Returns the timestamp specified in timestamp with the time zone specified in zone with precision p:

name: word_stem
doc: Returns the stem of word in the English language.

name: xxhash64
doc: Computes the xxHash64 hash of binary.

name: year
doc: Returns the year from x.

name: year_of_week
doc: Returns the year of the ISO week from x.

name: yow
doc: This is an alias for year_of_week().

name: zip
doc: Merges the given arrays, element-wise, into a single array of rows. The M-th element of the N-th argument will be the N-th field of the M-th output element. If the arguments have an uneven length, missing values are filled with NULL.

name: zip_with
signature: zip_with(array(T), array(U), function(T, U, R)) → array(R)
doc: Merges the two given arrays, element-wise, into a single array using function. If one array is shorter, nulls are appended at the end to match the length of the longer array, before applying function.
